skill.name: global-conventions
skill.description: Apply shared development conventions for project structure, version control, configuration, reviews, testing, feature flags, changelogs, and documentation.
skill.summary: Guides consistent development practices across a project team.
skill.author: DevanB

security_audit.summary: The weak cryptography findings at SKILL.md lines 3 and 8 are false positives from ordinary prose, with no cryptographic instruction present. The path traversal findings at SKILL.md line 28 are confirmed because the skill directs the agent to a relative path outside the skill directory. No evidence found of malicious intent, network access, command execution, or prompt injection.

medium_findings.title: Path Traversal Reference Leaves Skill Directory
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md line 28 links to ../../../agent-os/standards/global/conventions.md. This directs the agent outside the packaged skill directory, which can expose unreviewed local instructions or fail when the referenced file is absent.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The ../../../ sequence is directly present in the referenced location. The risk is contextual because it is a documentation link, not executable code.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Finding Is False Positive
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptography at SKILL.md lines 3 and 8, but those lines contain descriptive text about development conventions. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are plain Markdown prose and contain no crypto implementation or recommendation. This strongly supports dismissal of the blocker finding.

dangerous_patterns.title: Relative Parent Directory Traversal
dangerous_patterns.description: The Markdown reference on SKILL.md line 28 uses ../../../ to leave the skill directory. Marketplace packaging should avoid references to files outside the audited skill contents.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The traversal sequence is explicit and repeated in the path. The impact is limited because it appears in a Markdown link rather than a file operation.

content.value_statement: Teams lose time when structure, reviews, and release practices differ across contributors. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code apply consistent project conventions.
actual_capabilities: Guides project file and directory organization decisions.
Supports branch, commit message, and pull request consistency.
Helps document environment variables and configuration practices.
Frames code review expectations for authors and reviewers.
Guides testing, feature flag, changelog, and release note practices.
Helps document dependency choices and contribution guidelines.
limitations: It provides guidance and does not enforce repository rules automatically.
It depends on project-specific standards supplied by the user or repository.
It does not replace security, legal, or compliance review.
Its linked external conventions file may be unavailable in packaged form.

use_cases.title: Align Team Development Practices
use_cases.description: Use the skill to apply consistent project structure, review, testing, and release conventions across a team.

use_cases.title: Prepare Cleaner Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Use the skill to shape branches, commits, documentation, and review notes before opening a pull request.

use_cases.title: Maintain Project Documentation
use_cases.description: Use the skill to update setup notes, dependency rationale, changelogs, and contribution guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Review Project Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this project layout and suggest changes that match our shared development conventions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for organizing files and directories.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Pull Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me prepare this change for review, including branch naming, commit message, tests, and pull request notes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request before submitting a change.

prompt_templates.title: Document Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these environment variables and configuration files, then draft setup documentation that follows team conventions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for onboarding and operations documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Release Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a release preparation checklist covering feature flags, tests, changelog updates, dependency notes, and review responsibilities.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for coordinated release planning.

output_examples.input: Review a new service folder before the team adopts it.
output_examples.output: A concise structure review with recommended folder names, documentation gaps, and consistency issues to resolve.

output_examples.input: Prepare notes for a pull request that changes configuration behavior.
output_examples.output: A pull request outline with change summary, testing notes, configuration impact, and reviewer focus areas.

output_examples.input: Update release notes after enabling a feature flag.
output_examples.output: A release note draft that explains the flag status, user impact, testing coverage, and rollback consideration.

best_practices: Provide the repository standards or existing examples before asking for guidance.
Ask for concrete review output, such as checklists, summaries, or documentation drafts.
Confirm generated conventions with maintainers before applying broad workflow changes.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill as the only authority for security-sensitive workflow decisions.
Do not apply generic conventions when the repository already has stricter documented rules.
Do not ask it to rewrite team process without including current constraints and ownership.

faq.question: What does this skill help standardize?
faq.answer: It helps standardize project structure, version control, configuration, reviews, testing, feature flags, changelogs, and documentation.

faq.question: Does it modify files automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and drafts. The agent may edit files only when the user asks for changes.

faq.question: Can it create pull request content?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help draft summaries, testing notes, reviewer guidance, and release context.

faq.question: Is it specific to one programming language?
faq.answer: No. The guidance focuses on team workflow and repository conventions rather than language syntax.

faq.question: What context should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide existing standards, repository layout, branch rules, review expectations, and release requirements.

faq.question: Can it replace human review?
faq.answer: No. It can improve consistency, but maintainers should approve process, security, and release decisions.
